content: validdata  Table of contents Prototype: validdata(data_container, type) Return type: boolean Description: Validates a JSON container from data_container and returns true if the contents are valid JSON. This function is intended to be expanded with functionality for validating CSV and YAML files eventually, mirroring readdata() . If type is JSON , it behaves the same as validjson() . Arguments: data_container : string - String to validate as JSON - in the range: .* type : - Type of data to validate - one of JSON Example: Run: code bundle agent main { vars : " json_string " string => ' { "test" : [ 1 , 2 , 3 ] } ' ; reports : "This JSON string is valid!" if => validdata ( " $(json_string) " , "JSON" ); "This JSON string is not valid." unless => validdata ( " $(json_string) " , "JSON" ); } Output: code R: This JSON string is valid! See also: readdata() , validjson() History: Was introduced in 3.16.0.
